tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. IMPORTANT: Comments are subject to validation constraints enforced by the MCP server - maximum 65536 characters for the complete comment (including footer which is added automatically), 10 mentions (@username), and 50 links. Exceeding these limits will result in an immediate error with specific guidance. NOTE: By default, this tool requires discussions:write permission. If your GitHub App lacks Discussions permission, set 'discussions: false' in the workflow's safe-outputs.add-comment configuration to exclude this permission. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 comment(s) can be added.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation. CONSTRAINTS: The complete comment (your body text + automatically added footer) must not exceed 65536 characters total. Maximum 10 mentions (@username), maximum 50 links (http/https URLs). A footer (~200-500 characters) is automatically appended with workflow attribution, so leave adequate space. If these limits are exceeded, the tool call will fail with a detailed error message indicating which constraint was violated.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). If omitted, the tool auto-targets the issue, PR, or discussion that triggered this workflow. Auto-targeting only works for issue, pull_request, discussion, and comment event triggers — it does NOT work for schedule, workflow_dispatch, push, or workflow_run triggers. For those trigger types, always provide item_number explicitly, or the comment will be silently discarded.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 5 label(s) can be added.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the issue or PR that triggered this workflow. Only works for issue or pull_request event triggers. For schedule, workflow_dispatch, or other triggers, item_number is required — omitting it will silently skip the label operation.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

content.text:
{"number":56957,"title":"Optimize http-client-csharp-mgmt CI to only regenerate management SDKs (Azure.ResourceManager.*)","body":"## Problem

When changes are made to `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt`, the CI regeneration step regenerates **all** TypeSpec-enabled SDKs (both data-plane and management). This is unnecessarily slow because `http-client-csharp-mgmt` only affects management-plane SDKs (those starting with `Azure.ResourceManager`).

In contrast, `http-client-csharp` is the base generator for both data-plane and mgmt, so regenerating all SDKs on its changes is correct.

### Current Behavior

Both `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/ci.yml` and `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/ci.yml` use the same regeneration pipeline template (`archetype-typespec-emitter.yml`) with identical parameters. The matrix generation in `eng/common/scripts/New-RegenerateMatrix.ps1` collects **all** `/sdk/*` directories with `tsp-location.yaml`, with no filtering by SDK type.

**Flow:**
1. CI triggers `archetype-typespec-emitter.yml`
2. `New-RegenerateMatrix.ps1` enumerates all TypeSpec-enabled SDKs under `/sdk`
3. `Update-dotnet-GeneratedSdks` (in `eng/scripts/Language-Settings.ps1`) regenerates every SDK in the matrix
4. No distinction is made between mgmt and data-plane SDKs

### Expected Behavior

- **`http-client-csharp` changes** → regenerate **all** TypeSpec-enabled SDKs (correct, since it's the base for both data-plane and mgmt generators)
- **`http-client-csharp-mgmt` changes** → regenerate **only** management SDKs (`Azure.ResourceManager.*`)

### Suggested Approach

Filter the regeneration matrix for `http-client-csharp-mgmt` CI to only include directories whose package name starts with `Azure.ResourceManager`. Possible options:

1. **Add an emitter-type parameter** to `archetype-typespec-emitter.yml` and `New-RegenerateMatrix.ps1`. The mgmt CI would pass a filter (e.g., `SdkFilter=Azure.ResourceManager*`) to restrict which SDK directories are included in the matrix.

2. **Implement `Get-dotnet-DirectoriesForGeneration`** in `eng/scripts/Language-Settings.ps1` (currently missing, causing fallback to all directories) with support for filtering by SDK type.

3. **Filter in the CI yml** by passing additional arguments from `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/ci.yml` to the template.

### Impact

This would reduce the regeneration time for mgmt-only changes by skipping all data-plane SDKs, which are unaffected by management generator changes.

### Relevant Files

- `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/ci.yml`
- `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/ci.yml`
- `eng/common/pipelines/templates/archetype-typespec-emitter.yml`
- `eng/common/scripts/New-RegenerateMatrix.ps1`
- `eng/scripts/Language-Settings.ps1` (`Update-dotnet-GeneratedSdks`)","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/56957","user":{"login":"live1206","id":5196139,"profile_url":"https://github.com/live1206","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/5196139?v=4"},"author_association":"MEMBER","reactions":{"total_count":1,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":1},"created_at":"2026-03-11T08:45:38Z","updated_at":"2026-03-11T08:45:38Z"}

{"color":"ffeb77","description":"This issue is related to a management package.","id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxMTc4NDQ4MTEx","name":"Mgmt"}

arguments.body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This is a well-described feature request to optimize CI for `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt` so it only regenerates management-plane SDKs (`Azure.ResourceManager.*`) rather than all TypeSpec-enabled SDKs. The reporter correctly identifies that the mgmt generator only affects `Azure.ResourceManager.*` packages, making full regeneration wasteful.

**Type:** Feature Request / CI Optimization
**Affected area:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt`, `eng/common/pipelines/templates/archetype-typespec-emitter.yml`, `eng/common/scripts/New-RegenerateMatrix.ps1`, `eng/scripts/Language-Settings.ps1`

<details>
<summary>📋 Technical Analysis</summary>

### Root Cause
`New-RegenerateMatrix.ps1` collects **all** `/sdk/*` directories containing `tsp-location.yaml` with no discrimination between data-plane and management SDKs. Both `http-client-csharp/ci.yml` and `http-client-csharp-mgmt/ci.yml` use `archetype-typespec-emitter.yml` with identical parameters, causing the mgmt CI to regenerate hundreds of data-plane SDKs unnecessarily.

### Key Insight
- `http-client-csharp` (base generator) → changes affect **both** data-plane and mgmt → full regeneration is correct
- `http-client-csharp-mgmt` (mgmt-only generator) → changes only affect `Azure.ResourceManager.*` → should filter to only those SDKs

</details>

<details>
<summary>🛠️ Suggested Implementation Options</summary>

The issue author has outlined three approaches; here is a technical assessment:

**Option 1 (Recommended): Add `SdkFilter` parameter to `archetype-typespec-emitter.yml` and `New-RegenerateMatrix.ps1`**
- Minimal invasive change
- `New-RegenerateMatrix.ps1` would accept an optional `SdkFilter` (e.g., `Azure.ResourceManager*`) and use it to filter the `tsp-location.yaml` directories
- `http-client-csharp-mgmt/ci.yml` passes `SdkFilter: Azure.ResourceManager*` to the template

**Option 2: Implement `Get-dotnet-DirectoriesForGeneration` in `eng/scripts/Language-Settings.ps1`**
- This function is apparently missing, causing a fallback to "all directories"
- Implementing it with a filter parameter would be clean and language-agnostic

**Option 3: Filter in the CI YAML itself**
- Less reusable but simplest short-term fix

### How to identify mgmt vs data-plane SDKs
A package is management if `tsp-location.yaml` resolves to a project folder whose name starts with `Azure.ResourceManager` or `Microsoft.Azure.Management`. Alternatively, the `tspconfig.yaml`'s `emitter-output-dir` path can be checked for the `Azure.ResourceManager.*` pattern.

</details>

<details>
<summary>✅ Suggested Sub-Tasks / Checklist</summary>

- [ ] Audit how many SDKs are currently in the matrix for mgmt CI (to quantify the improvement)
- [ ] Choose one of the three implementation approaches
- [ ] Implement the filter in `New-RegenerateMatrix.ps1` (and/or `Get-dotnet-DirectoriesForGeneration`)
- [ ] Update `archetype-typespec-emitter.yml` to accept and pass through the filter parameter (if Option 1)
- [ ] Update `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/ci.yml` to pass the `Azure.ResourceManager*` filter
- [ ] Test that the filtered matrix correctly includes all `Azure.ResourceManager.*` SDKs and excludes data-plane SDKs
- [ ] Ensure `http-client-csharp/ci.yml` is unchanged (still regenerates all SDKs)

</details>

<details>
<summary>🔗 Relevant Resources</summary>

- [`eng/common/scripts/New-RegenerateMatrix.ps1`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/eng/common/scripts/New-RegenerateMatrix.ps1) — Matrix generation script to modify
- [`eng/common/pipelines/templates/archetype-typespec-emitter.yml`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/eng/common/pipelines/templates/archetype-typespec-emitter.yml) — Pipeline template
- [`eng/scripts/Language-Settings.ps1`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/eng/scripts/Language-Settings.ps1) — `Update-dotnet-GeneratedSdks` function
- [`eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/ci.yml`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/ci.yml) — Mgmt CI definition

</details>

> **Owners:** @live1206 @ArcturusZhang @ArthurMa1978 (per CODEOWNERS for `%CodeGen %Mgmt`)
